title: Stelis resupinata
taxonomy: Stelis resupinata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises approximately 30,000 species of orchids, which are one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Stelis resupinata is also classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large and widespread subfamily of orchids. Further classification places Stelis resupinata within the tribe Epidendreae, and the genus Stelis. The species is further classified within the subgenus Stelis.
uses: Stelis resupinata has been reported to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin infections. In some regions, the plant is used as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Stelis resupinata has also been studied for its potential antifungal and antibacterial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and safety.
benefits: Stelis resupinata, a species of orchid, is reported to have potential health benefits due to its unique chemical composition.
